chapter_title.en: Chapter 49 – The Found and the Lost Properties

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/115)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/115)
text: لَا بَأْسَ بِلُقَطَةِ الْعَصَا وَ الشِّظَاظِ وَ الْوَتِدِ وَ الْحَبْلِ وَ الْعِقَالِ وَ أَشْبَاهِهِ
قَالَ وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) لَيْسَ لِهَذَا طَالِبٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad, from Hareyz,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘There is no problem with the found staff, and the splinters, and the pegs, and the ropes, and the harnesses, and the likes of it’.
He (the narrator) said, ‘And Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There are no seekers for these’.
en.sarwar: 15. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad from Hariz who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘There is no offense in picking up a stick, pieces of wood used in the saddle of camels, stage, ropes, and harnesses and similar items. He (the Imam) then said, ‘Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, “Such things are not wanted items.’”” (Consult fatwa also.)
